Figure B suffers from an incorrect viewing azimuth that renders its layout and labels unreadable. Although both figures struggle with correctly conveying the underlying mathematical concept regarding the epipolar plane, Figure A is vastly superior in presentation and interactivity.

geometry.rationale: Figure A fakes the perspective by drawing flattened 2D shapes in a single plane, whereas Figure B accurately reconstructs the true 3D spatial relationships and geometry of the cameras and image planes.

interactivity.rationale: Figure A features highly functional direct 3D manipulation of the lifted point alongside its slider, whereas Figure B contains a ReferenceError (calling an undefined `updateLabels` function) that breaks its rendering and interactivity.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure A closely replicates the layout, colors, dashed lines, curved arrows, and text positioning of the original, whereas Figure B is completely disjointed and lacks key visual elements like the red dots.

labels.rationale: Figure A accurately reproduces all labels with good size and placement, whereas Figure B has tiny, cluttered labels and includes a typo.

concept.rationale: Figure B contains a syntax/reference error (calling the removed updateLabels function) which breaks the rendering, while Figure A correctly implements the conceptual interactions and 3D dragging.

aggregator.explanation: Four out of five dimension agents strongly prefer Figure A, primarily because Figure B contains a ReferenceError that breaks its rendering and interactivity. Although Figure B attempts a more rigorous 3D geometric setup, Figure A functions properly, remains highly faithful to the original visual design, and successfully implements the conceptual interactions.

geometry.rationale: Figure A models the camera's image plane correctly as being perpendicular to the optical axis pitched downwards towards the scene, whereas Figure B forces the image plane to be perfectly vertical, which is less geometrically representative of typical textbook homography setups.

interactivity.rationale: Figure A provides more comprehensive controls, including 2D sliders for the point's position on the plane (X and Y), whereas Figure B restricts the point movement to a single dimension.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure B more accurately reproduces the geometric projection, correctly aligns the coordinate axes with the plane corners, and matches the rotated text labels of the reference.

labels.rationale: Figure A includes all the required labels from the reference (including 'Camera center' and the 'Y' axis), whereas Figure B completely omits them.

concept.rationale: Figure A provides full 2D movement for the point on the plane and correctly orients the camera to look at the plane, whereas Figure B restricts point movement to a single axis that coincides with the tilt hinge, defeating the visual effect of perspective foreshortening.

aggregator.explanation: Figure A provides a much more accurate geometric representation and interactivity, allowing full 2D movement of the projected point. It also correctly includes all necessary labels and properly orients the camera's image plane, whereas Figure B artificially restricts point movement and misses crucial labels.

geometry.rationale: Figure B constructs the dashed projection rays using solid 3D cylinder segments rather than 1px WebGL dashed lines (which suffer from thinness/aliasing), and its L-block profile is a single continuous shape rather than two intersecting boxes which often produce z-fighting or internal edges in the projection.

interactivity.rationale: Figure B provides real-time value readouts for its sliders and includes an additional meaningful animation step in its interactive walkthrough.

faithfulness.rationale: Figure B correctly places the projection plane on the left and the object on the right, matching the reference's composition, colors, and axis orientation, whereas Figure A reverses the entire layout.

labels.rationale: Figure B includes all required labels, including 'Projection plane', correctly placed, whereas Figure A is missing 'Projection plane' and has misaligned axis labels.

concept.rationale: Figure B correctly models the 3D block as a unified L-shaped prism, resulting in a clean and mathematically accurate projection without internal overlapping lines, whereas Figure A uses two intersecting boxes which creates an inaccurate artifact in the 2D projection.

aggregator.explanation: Figure B is the unanimous winner due to its faithful layout, correctly placing the projection plane and object to match the reference. It utilizes superior geometry by modeling the L-block as a unified prism to avoid internal overlapping lines, and provides complete labels alongside enhanced interactivity.
